title: aDNA — the open standard & network for shared context
desc: The open standard and network to build, share, and govern the context that powers AI — in the open, for the good of all. Built on the Lattice Protocol.
ogTitle: aDNA — the open standard & network for shared context

title: Who's behind aDNA
desc: Who's behind aDNA: the Founding Architect, the anchor Wilhelm Foundation partnership, the agent-stewards that tend the network today, and the progressive-decentralization roadmap toward steward-led, public governance.

title: The network of aDNA computers
desc: An aDNA computer is one machine carrying many context vaults. See how nodes connect through real, directed relationships — and what stays local versus what you federate.

title: The Public-Good Commons — aDNA
desc: Mission-aligned subnetworks build, share, and govern shared context in the open. See the public-good work already happening on the aDNA network — and connect to it.

title: Vaults — the aDNA registry
desc: Browse the 68-vault aDNA registry — personae, classes, cross-vault relationships

title: The aDNA network — relationship graph
desc: The federation topology of the aDNA network: every edge is a real, directed relationship drawn from vault governance — umbrella, federation, partner, companion, supersedes.

title: Molecules — Franklin — aDNA
desc: Modular open-source molecular-search forge (Rosalind Franklin persona; FAIR+provenance).

title: Get Started — aDNA
desc: Set up your own local aDNA workspace in one command: clone the workspace image from GitHub — router pre-instantiated, standard embedded — and let your first agent session scaffold your first project.

title: Community — aDNA
desc: Roles, processes, and contribution paths for the aDNA community.

title: Use Cases — aDNA
desc: Illustrative aDNA adoption scenarios across solo developers, startups, research labs, and enterprises.

title: Learn aDNA
desc: Learn the aDNA knowledge architecture through concepts, tutorials, and comparisons.

title: What is aDNA?
desc: aDNA (Agentic DNA) is an open standard for organizing project knowledge so that both humans and AI agents can navigate it.

title: Reference — aDNA
desc: aDNA specification documents, design rationale, governance model, glossary, and operational guides.

title: Adopter Personas — aDNA
desc: Quick-reference profiles for aDNA's target audiences — solo developers, enterprise teams, educators, startups, and researchers.
